results.title: Tropfest #9 2001: 16 finalists
results.brief_description: The Sydney Film Festival, Tropfest, is the world’s largest short film festival, held one night in February each year at the Domain, Royal Botanic Gardens, Sydney. The aim is to showcase work from young and emerging filmmakers, providing them with a festival atmosphere to screen their works to peers. In an attempt to encourage production, not just the exhibition of films, the festival has a Tropfest Signature Item, announced a few months prior to the festival. Each film must contain the TSI in whatever form the filmmaker sees fit. In 2001, the TSI is ‘horn’ and the interpretations leap from, ‘The French’, to something a lot more personal. This tape contains 16 finalists which includes: “A Matter of life” (dir. Jennifer Perrott); “Tickler” (dir. Clayton Jacobson); “Muffled love” (dir. Rene Hernandez); “The Lighter” (dir. Patrick Hughes); “Just not cricket” (dir. Matt Wheeldon); “The Art of farewell” (dir. David Napier); “Beep” (dir. Eddie Edwards); “Like it is” (dirs. Chris Begley, Tony D’Aquino); “Home straight” (dir. Mickey Power); “Chopper” (dir. Bernard Derriman); “A Day at the zoo” (dir. Matthew Gidney); “Playin’ hard to get” (dir. Tim Bullock); “Boambee return” (dir. Liz Crosby); “Burn” (dir. Michael Dunning); “The Cutting room floor” (dir. Steve Baker); “Ride on” (dir. Nick Wyles).

results.title: Trik film three
results.creator_credit: George Griffin and Eccentric Circle Cinema Workshop
results.brief_description: A film experiment which uses one of the oldest methods of simple animation. The artist draws a series of pictures of a simple set of actions in progression. These are then stacked in the hand and flipped through to form a moving image. Useful for film making classes. Without commentary.

results.title: Trigger happy Harry
results.creator_credit: John B. Sandstone and Business Films
results.brief_description: Through the experiences of a careless and ignorant hunter, the safe and proper ways of handling a gun are taught. The National Rifle Association’s pointers for safety are covered: point the muzzle in a safe direction; is that gun loaded?; be sure of your target before you shoot; be sure of your backstop; know your gun and ammunition.

results.title: Triangle and anchor: Chelsea porcelain from the Williamsburg Collection
results.creator_credit: Gene Bjerke and Arthur L. Smith
results.brief_description: Traces the history of porcelain from China in the 9th century to Chelsea, England in the mid-eighteenth century. Discusses the various factory marks used on Chelsea porcelain and shows the development from simple white tableware to elaborately coloured and gilded dishes and figurines

results.title: Tree of wealth
results.brief_description: A survey of the uses to which the coconut is put in South India. No part of either the tree or its fruit is wasted and a living is provided for the entire countryside. Products of the tree include spun fibre; vases; brooms; fuel; choddy; oil and milk.

results.title: Treasures of the earth
results.brief_description: Highlights some of the new techniques being used in exploration, transporting and handling of natural resources in order to meet the increasing needs of modern society. Demonstrates new methods employing electronics, computers, lasers, aircraft, heavy machinery, and seismic equipment in locating additional resources on land and beneath the seas. Industrial promotional film for Litton Industries.

results.title: Treasures from the land: the crafts of New Zealand
results.creator_credit: George Andrews and George Andrews Productions
results.brief_description: Introduces New Zealand crafts which are bsed on cultural heritage, and for which materials are obtained from the environment. Narrated by Lisa Harrow.

results.title: Treasure Island
results.creator_credit: Warwick Gilbert, Roz Phillips and Burbank Films Australia
results.brief_description: When an old sea dog Billy Bones dies at the “Admiral Benbow Inn”, Jim Hawkins and Dr. Liversey come into possession of a map revealing the where- abouts of treasure hidden by the infamous Captain Kidd. They set out on the sailing ship “Hispaniola” to find the hoard and make their fortunes. In the great tradition of swashbuckling adventure stories Robert Louis Stevenson’s tale has taken its place among the best known and most widely read by children of all ages. Ross Higgins stars as the voice of Long John Silver and brings this animated video to life.

results.title: Traffic control
results.brief_description: The basic principles of traffic control - chiefly by manual signals - at busy intersections. It is intended to assist in the instruction of traffic control officers who deal with London’s traffic problems

results.title: Toyland revolt
results.creator_credit: H Tyrlova, T.R. Sadek, J Novotny and Czechoslovakia Film Corporation, Documentary Film Department, Section Zlin
results.brief_description: A combination of live actors and animated puppets tell the story of the revolt of the toys when an S.S. man invades a toymaker’s shop and forces the old man to flee. The toys unite in their struggle to defeat the evils of Nazism. Cast: Edvard Linkers and Jindra Laznicka.
